title: Modern Five-Board Bench
summary: A modern low-back bench from exactly five 2x6 boards and one box of screws. Two steps, two identical leg sets, no angles anywhere.
description:
This bench is an exercise in restraint: five 8-foot 2x6s, thirteen cuts, no angles, and a design that gets its modern look from proportion instead of ornament. The leg sets are stacked 2x6 slabs — chunky, flat-faced, and screwed together three per joint — and the seat and low back are full-width 48" boards.

With only two assembly steps, the build quality lives entirely in the prep: pick the five straightest 2x6s in the store (crowned boards ruin a design this minimal, because there's nothing else to look at), and take the finishing tip seriously — stain the boards BEFORE cutting and assembly, then touch up the cut ends. A slat bench is all corners and gaps after it's together; pre-finishing is half the labor for twice the result.

The two leg sets must be identical — clamp them together and compare before the top goes on. That's the whole quality control step, and the difference between a bench and a rocker.

tools.note: Thirteen straight crosscuts. That's the whole cut list.
Sand before pre-staining — mill glaze on 2x6s blotches stain.

materials.name: 2x6, 8 ft
materials.note: Sight down every board and reject crowns — the design is too minimal to hide a bad one.

materials.name: Self-tapping wood screws, #9 x 2-3/4", star drive
Exterior wood stain
materials.note: Deck/fence-rated. Apply to the boards BEFORE cutting; touch up ends after.

cutList.note: Run full height to carry the low backrest.

steps.title: Build two identical leg sets
steps.body:
Stack and screw each leg set per the diagram — the tall back leg with three 16-1/2" supports — using three screws per joint. Three, not two: a two-screw joint in wide 2x6 faces can still pivot; three triangulate it dead.

Build the second set clamped against the first, matching edge for edge. Identical leg sets are the entire structural story of this bench.
materials: Self-tapping wood screws, #9 x 2-3/4", star drive

steps.title: Lay out the legs and add the boards
steps.body:
Stand the leg sets on a flat surface, spaced so the 48" boards land flush at the ends, and screw down the seat slats and the back slat — three screws per joint into each leg set, even gaps between slats.

Sit-test it, touch up the cut ends with stain, and put it by the front door, at the foot of a bed, or against the fence. A design this quiet works everywhere.
materials: Self-tapping wood screws, #9 x 2-3/4", star drive

images.alt: Minimal modern wood bench with low back built from five 2x6 boards
